questions.text: Which instrument is used to measure temperature?
questions.explanation: A thermometer is used to measure temperature. It works on the principle of thermal expansion.

questions.text: Heat flows from hot object to cold object.
questions.explanation: Heat always flows from a region of higher temperature to a region of lower temperature until thermal equilibrium is reached.

questions.correctAnswer: Conduction is the transfer of heat through a material without the movement of particles.
questions.explanation: Conduction is the process of heat transfer in solids where heat moves from the hot end to the cold end without actual movement of particles.

questions.text: The normal human body temperature is ________°C.
questions.explanation: The normal human body temperature is 37°C (98.6°F).
